root_text.ud7.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud7.3:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyā manussā yebhuyyena kāmesu ativelaṁ sattā (…) rattā giddhā gathitā mucchitā ajjhosannā sammattakajātā kāmesu viharanti.
root_text.ud7.3:2.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁsu.
root_text.ud7.3:2.2: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud7.3:2.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud7.3:2.4: “idha, bhante, sāvatthiyā manussā yebhuyyena kāmesu ativelaṁ sattā rattā giddhā gathitā mucchitā ajjhosannā sammattakajātā kāmesu viharantī”ti.
root_text.ud7.3:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud7.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud7.3:1.3: Now at that time most of the people in Sāvatthī overly clung to sensual pleasures. Lustful, greedy, tied, infatuated, they lived completely addicted to sensual pleasures.
translation_text.ud7.3:2.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.ud7.3:2.2: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what was happening.
translation_text.ud7.3:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud7.3:4.1: “Clinging to sensual pleasures, <j>to the chains of the senses,
translation_text.ud7.3:4.2: blind to the faults of the fetters,
translation_text.ud7.3:4.3: clinging to the chain of the fetters,
translation_text.ud7.3:4.4: they’ll never cross the flood so vast.”

variant_text.ud7.3:1.3: (…) → (honti) (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | gathitā → gadhitā (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1, csp1ed) | ajjhosannā → ajjhopannā (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1, csp1ed)
